ND. take thou unto thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and. Abihu, Eleazar..and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.
CHAPTER XXIV. The divisions of the sons of Aaron. OW these are the divisions of the sons of Aaron. The sons of Aaron; Nadab and Abihu, Elenzar and Ithamar.
2 But Nadab and Abihu died hetare their father, and had no children; therefore Eleazar and Ithamar: executed the priest’s office.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Exodus 20:3:2
matches.x: [BEFORE ME.]184Literally, before My face. Hence I.E.’s comment. Its meaning is like that of And Haran died before the face of his father185J.P.S., in the presence of. Terah (Gen. 11:28), which means that Terah was with Haran and saw him die. Similarly, and Eleazar and Ithamar ministered in the priest’s office before the face of Aaron their father 186Translated literally. (Num. 3:4). The meaning of our verse thus is, it is unseemly for you to worship any other god along with Me since I am your Go

9 Now the first ‘lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph; the second to Geda- . liah, who with his brethren and sons were twelve;
The sun had risen upon the earth, when Lot came to Zoar.
Lot and his family left at daybreak. A short while later, the sun rose upon the earth, and Lot came to Tzoar.

At the advent of the morning... Rashi is saying that “Hashem caused to rain” does not relate to the preceding verse, “The sun had risen upon the earth, when Lot came to Zoar,” [to say] that only after Lot came to Zoar did it begin to rain. Rather, it means that “Hashem caused to rain” already before Lot came to Zoar, at the very advent of the morning, before the sun had even risen.

29. The. sons of. Merari;.. Mahli, Libni_ his. son, Shimei. his.son, Uzza his son,
The sons of Leivi [were] Gershon, Kehas and Merari.
29. The. sons of. Merari;.. Mahli, Libni_ his. son, Shimei. his.son, Uzza his son,

The problem: According to 3:18-20 (see Exod. 6:17-19), the three main Levitical clans of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari subdivide themselves into eight clans: two for Gershon (Libni and Shimei), four for Kohath (Amram, Izhar, Hebron, Uzziel), and two for Merari (Mahli and Mushi). Yet here five Levite clans are listed without any family connection to the traditional three: Libni, Hebron, Mahli, Mushi, Korah (the last, Korah, is a great-grandson of Levi, according to Exod. 6:21). Most scholars…

4 And there were more chief. men found of the sons of Eleazar than of the sons of Ithamar; and. thus were they divided. Among the sons of Eleazar there were sixteen chief men of the house of their fathers, and eight among the sons. of -Ithumar according to the house of their fathers.

4 And there were more chief. men found of the sons of Eleazar than of the sons of Ithamar; and. thus were they divided. Among the sons of Eleazar there were sixteen chief men of the house of their fathers, and eight among the sons. of -Ithumar according to the house of their fathers.
5. Now in. Shushan the palace there was a certain Jew, whose name was Mordecai, the son of Jair, the son ne ee the son of Kish, a Benjain:

Kish In the Jaredite genealogy the son of Corom and Father of Lib (Ether 1:18-19). Kish inherited a peaceful kingdom from his father, reigned during a time of peace, and passed the kingdom on to his son (Ether 10:17-18).
